title: Ten Minutes of Play Today
subtitle: A small activity for doing something with no purpose other than the doing of it
excerpt: A ten-minute activity for doing something that is not useful, productive, or necessary. For the life that has been running entirely on output and needs a gap of input for its own sake.

practice.whenToUse: Use on a day when everything has been productive, purposeful, and for someone else, and you want to do one thing that is just for the pleasure of it.

content.text: A single small activity for today: ten minutes of something with no purpose other than the doing of it. Not rest, not self-care with an outcome. Something that could be called play.

Choose one thing today that you would do even if nobody knew you were doing it, even if it produced nothing, even if it was slightly silly. Do it for ten minutes. Put the timer on. Give yourself permission to stop when it goes off, or carry on if the pull is there.
Making up words to a song in your head.
Playing a game on your phone without guilt.
Rearranging something just because it would look better.
Doing something with your hands that produces nothing useful.

Ten minutes of purposeless activity resets something that productive time cannot. The brain processes differently when it is not working toward an outcome. The gap it makes is not the same as rest. It is its own category, and it is underrepresented in most adult days.
Do the ten minutes. Notice how strange it feels, if it does. Do it again tomorrow.
